decks.notes: This deck focuses on controlling the game by removing opponent's threats with cards like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push, while also disrupting their hand with cards like Thoughtseize. The deck also utilizes creatures like Graveyard Trespasser and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to apply pressure on the opponent. The mana base is designed to support these cards efficiently, with lands like Mutavault and Overgrown Tomb. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent, utilizing a mix of removal, disruption, and threats to grind out a victory.

This Dimir Aggro deck focuses on using a combination of faerie creatures, removal spells, and powerful creatures to aggressively control the battlefield and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to apply early pressure with cheap creatures like Faerie Miscreant and Moon-Circuit Hacker, while also using disruption spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to deal with threats. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares helps to close out games quickly and efficiently. The deck's strategy revolves around being proactive and disrupting the opponent's strategies to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources and disrupting their game plan using powerful removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push. It also utilizes creatures like Graveyard Trespasser and Sentinel of the Nameless City to pressure the opponent and close out the game. The inclusion of cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Culling Ritual provides additional ways to control the board and gain an advantage. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by efficiently using its resources and maintaining control of the game.

The deck focuses on utilizing control elements such as counterspells, removal, and card draw to maintain board control and outvalue the opponent. It includes a mix of powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, along with support cards like Consult the Star Charts and Get Lost to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to control the game, stall until it can establish a strong board presence, and then close out the game with powerful finishers like March of Otherworldly Light and Ultima.

The deck focuses on utilizing various control elements such as removal spells, hand disruption, and graveyard manipulation to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually grinding out advantage with powerful creatures and card advantage engines. The deck aims to control the board and dictate the pace of the game, ultimately overwhelming the opponent with resilient threats and disruptive spells. It leverages a mix of removal, hand disruption, and graveyard interaction to dismantle opposing strategies and maintain a strong presence on the battlefield.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push, while also utilizing creatures with abilities that disrupt the opponent's game plan. It aims to grind out advantages over time with cards like Graveyard Trespasser and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The mana base is designed to support both the control elements and the creature threats, with options like Mutavault and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth. The deck also includes disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Duress to further disrupt the opponent's strategy, giving the pilot multiple paths to victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing a combination of removal, hand disruption, and powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Emeritus of Abundance. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while gradually gaining advantage and resource advantage through efficient removal and resource denial. The deck also includes a mix of lands to ensure consistent mana production and to support the deck's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind out a win through attrition and consistency.

The focus of this deck is to quickly apply pressure on the opponent through aggressive spells and creatures, utilizing a combination of direct damage, card draw, and tempo plays. The deck aims to efficiently deal damage to the opponent while maintaining a strong board presence and control over the game. With a mix of burn spells, counterspells, and creatures with evasion, the deck seeks to outpace the opponent and secure victory through a combination of burst damage and incremental advantage.

The key concept of this Dimir Aggro deck is to overwhelm your opponent with a mix of evasive threats, disruption, and removal. By utilizing powerful creatures like Floodpits Drowner, Spyglass Siren, and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares, along with key disruptors like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, the deck aims to apply pressure early and disrupt your opponent's game plan. With a balanced mix of aggression and control elements, this deck can quickly close out games while keeping your opponent on the back foot.

This deck focuses on aggressive gameplay with a mix of creatures, removal spells, and disruption to quickly overpower opponents. The key strategy involves playing efficient threats like Bloodtithe Harvester and Graveyard Trespasser while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The deck also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Liliana of the Veil and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to gain additional advantage in the game. Additionally, the deck includes synergistic cards like Fable of the Mirror-Breaker to maintain pressure on the opponent and close out games swiftly.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively attack the opponent with a variety of low-cost creatures and burn spells. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Emberheart Challenger, and Soul-Scar Mage, the deck can deal damage quickly and efficiently. Cards like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage provide additional removal and reach to finish off opponents. Mutavault and Den of the Bugbear offer utility and flexibility in combat. Ultimately, the key strategy is to apply pressure early and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using cards like Abrupt Decay, Fatal Push, and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also utilizes creatures like Badgermole Cub and Graveyard Trespasser to apply pressure on the opponent. Additionally, the inclusion of powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Emeritus of Abundance provides strong late-game threats. The deck is designed to outvalue and outlast opponents through a combination of removal, disruption, and powerful creatures.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push, while also utilizing creatures like Badgermole Cub and Sentinel of the Nameless City to maintain board presence. The deck aims to disrupt opponents' strategies with disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Culling Ritual, while also generating value through graveyard interactions with cards like Graveyard Trespasser and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The inclusion of utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth helps to give the deck flexibility in various matchups.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm your opponent with cheap aggressive creatures and burn spells. The deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early in the game with cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while also having access to efficient removal and tempo plays with cards like Burst Lightning and Boomerang Basics. By combining creature aggression with burn spells and tempo plays, this deck looks to win the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear, backed up by spells that provide direct damage and card advantage. By maintaining a fast and aggressive pace, the deck aims to pressure the opponent and close out the game before they have a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Monstrous Rage further enhance the deck's ability to maintain and push its advantage, ensuring a swift victory.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly build up board presence with low-cost creatures and spells, while also utilizing instant and sorcery spells to efficiently deal damage to the opponent. Cards like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Gran-Gran are used to put pressure on the opponent early on, while spells like Combustion Technique and Firebending Lesson help to clear blockers or deal direct damage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and burn spells to secure a quick victory.
